summary: Small Python tooling maintenance PR that made the repository easier to set up and use across platforms. It added a pinned requirements.txt for Python dependencies, ignored local Python virtualenv directories, and made diff_settings.py choose the devkitPPC objdump executable suffix based on the host platform instead of always assuming a Windows .exe binary.
what_changed: Added a Python section to .gitignore and ignored venv/ so local virtual environments are not accidentally committed.
Added requirements.txt with pinned Python package versions: argcomplete, capstone, colorama, coverage, cxxfilt, graphviz, pyelftools, python_Levenshtein, and watchdog.
Imported platform in diff_settings.py and added get_exe_suffix() to distinguish Linux and Windows executable naming.

Changed config["objdump_executable"] from always ending in powerpc-eabi-objdump.exe to appending get_exe_suffix(), allowing Linux to use powerpc-eabi-objdump without .exe and Windows to continue using .exe.
smart_moves: Kept the objdump path anchored to DEVKITPRO/devkitPPC/bin while only varying the executable suffix, minimizing behavioral change.
Pinned Python dependency versions in requirements.txt, improving reproducibility for scripts and tooling used by contributors.
Handled the common Linux and Windows dev environments explicitly rather than relying on contributors to manually edit diff_settings.py.
Ignored venv/ at the repository level, matching the new dependency-file workflow where contributors may create local virtual environments.
decomp_lessons: Decompilation tooling should avoid host-specific assumptions where possible; even a hardcoded .exe suffix can block Linux users from running compare/diff workflows.
For Python-based project tooling, a checked-in requirements.txt gives future contributors a concrete environment to reproduce instead of reverse-engineering dependencies from import errors.
Keep local environment artifacts such as virtualenv directories out of version control when adding Python setup support.
Small repo-root quality-of-life changes can improve contributor setup without touching game code or matching-sensitive source files.
naming_conventions: Used get_exe_suffix() as a simple helper name describing the platform-specific executable suffix.
Kept existing diff_settings.py config keys unchanged, including baseimg, myimg, mapfile, source_directories, map_format, build_dir, and objdump_executable.
Used the standard requirements.txt filename for Python dependency installation workflows.
Used venv/ as the ignored local virtual environment directory name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or code matching tactics were involved; this PR only affected Python/tooling setup.
The practical matching-adjacent effect was making the objdump executable path work on Linux as well as Windows, which supports diffing/comparison workflows.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
No explicit reviewer rationale was available beyond the title and diff.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modified diff_settings.py objdump_executable or platform handling.
Find PRs that updated requirements.txt dependency versions.
Find tooling PRs that improved Linux or Windows setup for compare/diff workflows.
Check whether get_exe_suffix() was later changed to raise an Exception object instead of a string.
